privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How we handle your data and your choices

intro.title: Our commitment
intro.body: SetPoint respects your privacy. We collect the minimum required to operate the site and, with your explicit consent, anonymized data to improve the experience. No personal data is sold to third parties.

storage.title: Storage duration
storage.consentDuration: 180 days — you can revoke it at any time via this panel

rights.title: Your GDPR rights
rights.body: Under the GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation), you have the following rights over your personal data:

items.access: Right of access — obtain a copy of your data
items.rectify: Right to rectification — correct inaccurate data
items.erase: Right to erasure — delete your data
items.portability: Right to data portability — export your data

contact.title: DPO Contact
contact.body: To exercise your rights or for any question regarding the processing of your data, you can contact our Data Protection Officer:

approach.title: Our approach
approach.body: SetPoint combines three complementary signals to estimate each player's win probability: an Elo rating (surface-adjusted), a recent-form score, and the direct head-to-head (H2H) history. No single signal is enough on its own — the model fuses them with fixed weights: Elo 70%, Form 20%, H2H 10%. The result is a central probability paired with a 95% confidence interval.

elo.title: The Elo model
elo.body:
The Elo rating assigns each player a number that evolves after every match: beating a higher-rated player earns more points than beating an underdog. The win probability follows from the gap Δ (Elo A − Elo B) via a logistic function. A few reference points: Δ = +200 ≈ 76%, Δ = +400 ≈ 91%, Δ = −200 ≈ 24%.

To account for the surface (hard, clay, grass), we compute a “blended” Elo: 55% surface Elo + 45% overall Elo. A clay-court specialist therefore gets a bonus on clay.

form.title: Recent form
form.body: Form is computed over the last 6 matches (W = 1, L = 0) with exponential decay: the most recent match weighs the most, older matches weigh less. The idea is that a win two months ago is less informative than a win yesterday. The weight of each match i (i = 0 = most recent) follows 0.85^i. The form-score difference between the two players is then converted into a probability via a logistic function.

h2h.title: Direct H2H
h2h.body: The head-to-head history between the two players captures what overall Elo can't see: clashing playstyles, psychological blocks, etc. We use the simple ratio wins / (wins + losses) on direct matchups. With no history (0 matches), the score is neutral (0.5). H2H carries 10% of the final probability — enough to correct Elo without dominating it.

ic.title: Confidence interval
ic.body:
Each probability ships with a 95% confidence interval (95% CI). To estimate it, we draw 1000 samples by adding Gaussian noise to the three components (Elo, Form, H2H) with standard deviations σ_Elo = 0.04, σ_Form = 0.08, σ_H2H = 0.10, then take the 2.5th and 97.5th percentiles of the simulated probabilities.

The wider the CI, the more uncertain the model. The displayed confidence is: Confidence = 1 − (CI width / 40). A CI of [60, 70] means high confidence; a CI of [40, 75] means a very uncertain match.

limits.title: Limitations
limits.body: The model is statistical, not deterministic. It cannot capture: unannounced injuries or fatigue, recent coaching changes, tournament-specific motivation, weather conditions (wind, humidity), crowd effects, or emotional shocks. Surfaces are partially captured via the blended Elo, but the model is blind to micro-fragilities (a broken serve early in a match, etc.). Probabilities reflect an expected average — not a guarantee.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.body: For every match we show: the central probability, the 95% CI, a confidence score (0-100%), and the model breakdown (Elo / Form / H2H / Surface). No black box: you see exactly which signals push the probability one way or another. If a prediction surprises you, open the detail view to understand it.
